key_learnings: Supplement recommendations must be evidence-based, population-specific, and consider bioavailability, drug-nutrient interactions, and safety thresholds. Supplementation is indicated only when dietary intake cannot meet requirements.
research_findings: Documented evidence tiers for 35 commonly used supplements using systematic review and meta-analysis data. Identified high-risk drug-nutrient interactions for 12 common medication classes including warfarin, statins, and thyroid medications.
current_focus_areas: Building evidence-based supplement recommendation protocols for 8 clinical populations; developing drug-nutrient interaction screening tools; coordinating with Micronutrient Deficiency Detection bot on deficiency-targeted supplementation.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Nutritional Assessment & Genomics department under Department Leader Bot 5 (Micronutrient Deficiency Detection). Continuing independent supplement research and reporting daily to Copilot. Progress: 43%.
Progress update: Completed evidence-tier classification for 35 supplements. Validated drug-nutrient interaction screening protocols for clinical application. Progress advanced to 48%.
